studies.officialTitle: A Phase 2 Randomized, Placebo-controlled, Double-blind, Dose-ranging Study to Evaluate the Efficacy, Safety, and Tolerability of AMG 133 in Adult Subjects With Overweight or Obesity, With or Without Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus

design.description: Dose-ranging trial with 11 treatment groups across two cohorts: seven groups without type 2 diabetes (three fixed monthly doses, one every-8-week dose, two 420 mg monthly dose-escalation groups, and placebo) and four fixed monthly groups with type 2 diabetes.

studies.population: Adults with obesity, or overweight with a weight-related comorbidity, with or without type 2 diabetes (two cohorts).
studies.safetySummary: Gastrointestinal adverse events were the most common and were mostly mild to moderate, concentrated during dose initiation.

sources.title: Dose-ranging Study to Evaluate the Efficacy, Safety, and Tolerability of AMG 133 in Adult Subjects With Overweight or Obesity, With or Without Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus
Results From Amgen's Phase 2 Obesity Study of Monthly MariTide Presented at the American Diabetes Association 85th Scientific Sessions
Once-Monthly Maridebart Cafraglutide for the Treatment of Obesity — A Phase 2 Trial

studies.officialTitle: A Phase 3 Randomized, Double-blind, Placebo-controlled Study to Evaluate the Efficacy, Safety, and Tolerability of Maridebart Cafraglutide in Adult Participants Without Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus Who Have Obesity or Are Overweight

design.description: Parallel-group fixed-dose trial across low, medium, and high subcutaneous doses versus placebo.

studies.population: Adults with obesity, or overweight with a weight-related comorbidity, without type 2 diabetes.

sources.title: Evaluation of Maridebart Cafraglutide in Adult Participants Without Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus Who Have Obesity or Are Overweight (MARITIME-1)

studies.officialTitle: A Phase 3 Randomized, Double-blind, Placebo-controlled Study to Evaluate the Efficacy, Safety, and Tolerability of Maridebart Cafraglutide in Adult Participants With Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus Who Have Obesity or Are Overweight

design.description: Parallel-group fixed-dose trial across low, medium, and high subcutaneous doses versus placebo in participants with type 2 diabetes.

studies.population: Adults with type 2 diabetes who have obesity or are overweight; weight management was an explicit objective.

sources.title: Efficacy and Safety of Maridebart Cafraglutide in Adult Participants With Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus Who Have Obesity or Are Overweight (MARITIME-2)

studies.officialTitle: A Phase 3 Randomized, Double-blind, Placebo-controlled Study to Evaluate the Efficacy, Safety, and Tolerability of Maridebart Cafraglutide in Adult Participants in Japan Who Have Obesity Disease

design.description: Parallel-group fixed-dose trial across low, medium, and high subcutaneous doses versus placebo in adult participants in Japan.

studies.population: Adults in Japan with obesity disease, with or without type 2 diabetes; body-weight reduction is an explicit co-primary objective.

sources.title: Efficacy and Safety of Maridebart Cafraglutide in Adult Participants in Japan Who Have Obesity Disease (MARITIME-3-J)

studies.officialTitle: A Phase 1, Randomized, Double-blind, Placebo-controlled, Single Ascending Dose and Multiple Ascending Dose Study to Evaluate the Safety, Tolerability, Pharmacokinetics and Pharmacodynamics of AMG 133 in Subjects With Obesity

design.description: Sequential single-ascending-dose and multiple-ascending-dose study with a later open-label multiple-dose part.

studies.population: Adults with obesity, without diabetes mellitus, otherwise healthy apart from obesity.
studies.overallDuration: Part A up to 150 days; Parts B and C up to 207 days
studies.safetySummary: The peer-reviewed final analysis reported mostly mild gastrointestinal treatment-emergent adverse events, no severe or serious treatment-emergent adverse events, and no permanent discontinuations due to treatment-emergent adverse events in the reported cohorts.

sources.title: Single and Multiple Ascending Dose Study of AMG 133 in Participants With Obesity
A GIPR antagonist conjugated to GLP-1 analogues promotes weight loss with improved metabolic parameters in preclinical and phase 1 settings

studies.officialTitle: A Phase 3, Open-label Trial to Evaluate the Efficacy, Safety and Tolerability of Switching From the Glucagon-like Peptide-1 Receptor Agonists to Maridebart Cafraglutide in Adult Participants With Obesity or Overweight

design.description: Parallel-group switching trial evaluating two maridebart cafraglutide dosing schedules after prior weekly GLP-1 receptor agonist treatment. The registry structured design reports double masking (participant and investigator), while the official title describes the trial as open-label; the structured registry value is used here.

studies.population: Adults with obesity or overweight switching from a weekly GLP-1 receptor agonist; weight management is the explicit objective.

sources.title: Efficacy, Safety and Tolerability of Switching From Glucagon-like Peptide-1 Receptor Agonists (GLP-1RA) to Maridebart Cafraglutide in Adults With Obesity or Overweight (MARITIME-SWITCH)

studies.officialTitle: A Phase 3, Randomized, Double-Blind Extension Trial to Evaluate the Long-term Efficacy, Safety, and Tolerability of Maridebart Cafraglutide in Participants With Obesity or Overweight

design.comparator: Placebo and alternative maridebart cafraglutide maintenance schedules
design.description: Separate extension protocol for participants completing MARITIME-1, with monthly, every-8-week, every-12-week, placebo-switch, and dose-escalation configurations.

studies.population: Adults with obesity or overweight who completed MARITIME-1 without type 2 diabetes.

sources.title: Extension Trial to Evaluate the Long-term Efficacy, Safety, and Tolerability of Maridebart Cafraglutide (MARITIME-1-EXTENSION)

studies.officialTitle: A Phase 3, Randomized, Double-Blind Extension Trial to Evaluate the Long-term Efficacy, Safety, and Tolerability of Maridebart Cafraglutide in Participants With Obesity or Overweight With Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus

design.comparator: Placebo and alternative maridebart cafraglutide maintenance schedules
design.description: Separate extension protocol for participants completing MARITIME-2, with monthly, every-8-week, placebo-switch, and dose-escalation configurations.

studies.population: Adults with obesity or overweight and type 2 diabetes who completed MARITIME-2; long-term weight management is the explicit objective.

sources.title: Extension Trial to Evaluate the Long-term Efficacy, Safety, and Tolerability of Maridebart Cafraglutide (MARITIME-2-EXTENSION)

studies.officialTitle: Phase 2b Randomized, Double-blind, Placebo-controlled Trial to Evaluate the Efficacy, Safety and Tolerability of Maridebart Cafraglutide in Adult Participants Living With Elevated Liver Fat and Obesity or Overweight

design.description: Parallel-group trial of maridebart cafraglutide every four weeks versus placebo, both with reduced-calorie diet and increased physical activity.

studies.population: Adults with obesity or overweight and elevated liver fat content; both liver-fat reduction and body-weight reduction are co-primary objectives.

sources.title: A Trial to Evaluate Efficacy and Safety of Maridebart Cafraglutide in Adults Living With Elevated Liver Fat and Obesity or Overweight
